evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly shows up/down/NS classification with dashed threshold lines and top-5 gene annotations; fully addresses the brief.
evidence.typography: Script uses default DejaVu Sans and does NOT set pdf.fonttype=42; PDF contains Type3 (bitmap) fonts, not selectable TrueType; no rcParams override present.
evidence.palette: Red (#D62728) for up-regulated, blue (#1F77B4) for down-regulated, grey (#AAAAAA) for NS — all colorblind-safe; directional coding is color-appropriate (no green/red confusion).
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout applied, no gaping whitespace, scatter points well distributed, legend placed upper-right without overlap; y-range appears sensible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure including thresholds and counts; legend is inside the plot upper-right; gene-index label caveat is explained; minor: no p-value method stated.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type3 (bitmap-outline) fonts due to missing pdf.fonttype=42 setting; text is not freely selectable/editable in a vector editor; scatter points are rasterized images embedded in PDF.
evidence.file_format: All required files present: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py — all non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numerical findings (up n=235, down n=230, NS n=2535), threshold values (|log2FC|>1, -log10(p)>2), explains dashed lines and gene-index labels.

overall_strengths: Clear, publication-ready volcano plot with correct 3-color classification, threshold lines, and top-5 gene annotations matching the brief exactly.
Caption is thorough with specific counts and threshold values; colorblind-safe palette with appropriate directional color coding.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype setting) means PDF fonts are rendered as Type3 bitmaps rather than editable TrueType glyphs, failing vector-text editability requirements.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout with no override to a publication-standard font (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif option).
